info.title: Indonesian Hoax News Dataset
info.subtitle: Official hoax clarification articles from the Indonesian Government (Kominfo).
info.description:
# Indonesian Hoax News (Berita Hoaks) Dataset

--- ### Context This dataset provides a comprehensive and structured collection of hoax news clarifications officially published by the Indonesian Ministry of Communication and Informatics (Kominfo) through its Digital Communication Directorate portal, `komdigi.go.id`. In an era of rampant misinformation, access to verified, machine-readable data from official sources is critical for researchers, data scientists, and developers aiming to build tools and models to combat its spread. This dataset was generated via a high-performance, parallel data crawler designed to be robust, efficient, and respectful to the source server. It directly interfaces with the site's backend API, ensuring data integrity and completeness.
### Key Features * **Official & Authoritative Source**: All data is sourced directly from the Indonesian government's official channel for hoax clarifications. * **Comprehensive Coverage**: Contains all available articles, spanning years of published content, with over 15,000 records. * **Rich Content**: Includes both the raw HTML (`body_html`) for structural analysis and the clean, extracted text (`body_text`) for immediate NLP tasks. * **Structured Metadata**: Provides essential metadata such as publication dates, view counts, categories, tags, and topics for deeper analysis. * **Ready for NLP**: The cleaned text field is pre-processed, making it ideal for immediate use in classification, topic modeling, named entity recognition (NER), and sentiment analysis models.
### Collection Methodology The dataset was collected using a custom Python script designed for high-throughput, ethical scraping. 1. **Endpoint Identification**: The crawler targets the website's public backend API, not the presentation-layer HTML, for superior data quality and efficiency. ``` https://web.komdigi.go.id/api/v1/contents/category/berita-hoaks ``` 2. **Parallel Processing**: A `ThreadPoolExecutor` was employed to fetch multiple pages concurrently, significantly reducing the total collection time from hours to minutes. A `requests.Session` object was used for connection pooling and header persistence. 3. **Anti-Crawling Evasion**: The script utilizes a complete and realistic set of browser headers (`User-Agent`, `Referer`, etc.) to ensure stable and uninterrupted access. 4. **Data Transformation**: Raw JSON responses were parsed, and the HTML content within the `body` field was cleaned using `BeautifulSoup` to generate the `body_text` column. The final structured data was then compiled into a Pandas DataFrame.
### Data Schema (Column Descriptions) | Column Name | Data Type | Description | | ---------------- | --------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `id` | Integer | The unique identifier for the article from the source database. | | `url` | String | The full, direct URL to the original article on the `komdigi.go.id` website. | | `title` | String | The complete title of the news clarification. | | `slug` | String | The URL-friendly version of the title. | | `published_at` | Timestamp | The exact date and time the article was published. | | `view_count` | Integer | The number of times the article has been viewed. | | `excerpt` | String | A short summary or excerpt of the article, if available. | | `body_html` | String | The raw HTML content of the article body. Useful for structural analysis. | | `body_text` | String | The cleaned, plain-text content of the article, stripped of all HTML tags. | | `main_image_url` | String | The URL of the main feature image associated with the article. | | `category` | String | The primary category, typically "Klarifikasi Hoaks". | | `tags` | String | A comma-separated list of tags associated with the article. | | `topics` | String | A comma-separated list of topics associated with the article. |
### Potential Use Cases * **Hoax Detection Models**: Train a classifier to distinguish between factual news and misinformation styles. * **Topic Modeling**: Analyze the most prevalent themes and categories of hoaxes over time (e.g., health, politics, financial scams). * **Trend Analysis**: Correlate hoax publication frequency with real-world events. * **Named Entity Recognition (NER)**: Identify frequently targeted individuals, organizations, and locations in misinformation campaigns. * **Educational Tools**: Develop applications or dashboards to visualize the landscape of misinformation in Indonesia.
### File Descriptions * `komdigi_hoaks.csv`: The complete dataset in CSV format, encoded in UTF-8-SIG for compatibility. * `komdigi_hoaks.json`: The complete dataset in JSON format, structured as a list of records.
### Acknowledgements & Disclaimer This dataset is provided for educational and research purposes only. Full credit and acknowledgement go to the **Kementerian Komunikasi dan Informatika Republik Indonesia (Kominfo)** for their vital work in providing these clarifications to the public. This dataset is not an official publication of the Indonesian government.
